  - person's voice type. expected values: soprano, mezzo-soprano, contralto, countertenor, tenor, baritone, bass (and derivatives)

  - the person who is speaking in this audio or video file
4. speaker (wd:P823 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "speech by", "spoken by":
  - person who is speaker for this event, ceremony, keynote, presentation or in a literary work
5. audio (wd:P51 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "audio file", "audio recording", "audio sample", "recording", "song", etc.:
  - relevant sound. If available, use a more specific property. Samples: "spoken text audio" (P989), "pronunciation audio" (P443)
6. audio recording of the subject's spoken voice (wd:P990 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "spoken voice"), also known as "audio clip", "recording of the subject's spoken voice", "speaking voice", "spoken voice", "voice recording":
  - audio file representing the speaking voice of a person; or of an animated cartoon or other fictitious character
7. produced sound (wd:P4733 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sound"), also known as "call", "makes call", "makes sound", "sound", "sound emitted", etc.:
  - item for the sound generated by the subject, for instance the cry of an animal
8. section (wd:P958 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sub voce"), also known as "article", "bibliography", "clause", "dictionary entry", "encyclopedia entry", etc.:
  - paragraph, or other kind of special indication to find information on a page or on a document (legal texts etc.)
9. honorific prefix (wd:P511 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "called"), also known as "called", "honorific title", "pre-nominal", "referred to as", "style prefix":
  - word or expression used before a name, in addressing or referring to a person
10. indigenous to (wd:P2341 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "spoken by"), also known as "native to", "originating from", "spoken by":
  - place or ethnic group where a language, art genre, cultural ... species or variety is found (or was originally found)
